headline: How The Corning (GLW) Narrative Is Shifting With AI Fiber Deals And Richer Valuation Assumptions
summary: Corning’s modeled fair value has been reset from about US$114.46 to about US$129.64, a shift of roughly US$15 per share that puts fresh attention on what is priced into the stock. Much of this move reflects how analysts are weighing the scale out and optical stories, with some tying higher targets to long term revenue pools while others stay cautious on execution, single customer risk and the timing of data center demand. As you read on, you will see how to track these changing assumptions...

headline: Corning (GLW) Is Up 9.3% After $6 Billion Meta AI Fiber Deal - Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: Corning Incorporated and Meta Platforms recently broke ground on a major expansion of Corning’s optical cable manufacturing capacity in Hickory, North Carolina, under a multiyear agreement worth up to US$6.00 billion to supply advanced fiber, cable, and connectivity solutions for AI data centers. This expansion deepens Meta’s role as an anchor customer while underscoring Corning’s growing U.S. manufacturing footprint in optical infrastructure critical to AI workloads. We’ll now examine how...

headline: If You Invested $1000 in Corning a Decade Ago, This is How Much It'd Be Worth Now
summary: Why investing for the long run, especially if you buy certain popular stocks, could reap huge rewards.

headline: Corning Meta Fiber Expansion Tests High Valuation And AI Growth Story
summary: Corning and Meta Platforms have begun construction on a major expansion of Corning’s optical cable manufacturing facilities in North Carolina. The project supports a multiyear partnership to supply next generation fiber for advanced AI data centers across the U.S. The expansion is intended to increase U.S. based production capacity for fiber used in AI focused data infrastructure. For investors watching Corning, NYSE:GLW, this new buildout comes as the stock trades at $147.92 with a return...
